role: Co-founder and Secretary-General of PJAK (Party of Free Life of Kurdistan)
bio: Abdul Rahman Haji Ahmadi is a prominent Iranian Kurdish political figure and one of the founders of [PJAK](/orgs/pjak) (Partiya Jiyana Azad a Kurdistanê — Party of Free Life of Kurdistan), established in 2004. He has served as the organisation's Secretary-General and is its main public spokesperson. PJAK is ideologically and organisationally linked to the [PKK](/orgs/pkk) and follows the democratic confederalism philosophy of Abdullah Öcalan. Haji Ahmadi has represented PJAK in international media and diplomatic channels, advocating for Kurdish self-governance within a decentralised Iranian political system. He operates from the Qandil Mountains along the Iraq-Iran border. Iran has designated him a terrorist and seeks his prosecution.
editorial_notes: Co-founder and Secretary-General of PJAK. Based in Qandil Mountains. Designated terrorist by Iran.
